title: Podman nests runtimes in cgroups when container is constantly restarting ending with "Main process exited, code=exited, status=219/CGROUP"
body:
### Issue Description

We are using privileged podman on RHEL9 so we cannot update it to the latest version (internal company policy).

When running container that is constantly restarting because of an internal configuration failure the runtimes directories inside cgroup folder nests indefinitely. In below example we run an nginx container with an error inside  it's configuration file.

Container quadlet file:
```bash
[Container]
ContainerName=lb-nginx
HostName=lb-nginx
Image=docker.io/library/nginx-custom:1.26-alpine
LogDriver=journald
LogOpt=tag=podman-lb-nginx
LogOpt=max-size=50mb
LogOpt=path=None
Network=lb-nginx.network
PublishPort=81:81/tcp
PublishPort=162:162/udp
PublishPort=514:514/udp
PublishPort=2055:2055/udp
PublishPort=6514:6514/tcp
Volume=/opt/containers/projects/lb-nginx/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
Volume=/opt/containers/projects/lb-nginx/conf.d:/etc/nginx/conf.d:ro
Volume=/opt/containers/projects/lb-nginx/log:/var/log/nginx/:Z
Volume=/opt/containers/projects/lb-nginx/snippets:/etc/nginx/snippets:ro
Volume=/opt/containers/projects/ssl/certificates:/certs:ro
PodmanArgs=--restart on-failure
PodmanArgs=--security-opt label=disable
[Unit]
Description=lb-nginx
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target
[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

Network quadlet file:
```bash
[Network]
NetworkName=lb-nginx
Internal=false
Options=isolate=True
```

Cgroup runtime buildup:

```bash
# tree /sys/fs/cgroup/system.slice/lb-nginx.service/
--- output omitted ---
307 directories, 22792 files

# find /sys/fs/cgroup/system.slice/lb-nginx.service/ -name cgroup.procs -exec sh -c 'echo {}' \;
/sys/fs/cgroup/system.slice/lb-nginx..procs
```

This is also reflected in `systemctl status lb-nginx`:

```bash
● lb-nginx.service - lb-nginx
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/containers/systemd/lb-nginx.container; generated)
     Active: active (running) since Thu 2026-02-19 12:37:39 CET; 8min ago
   Main PID: 80970 (conmon)
      Tasks: 12 (limit: 10516)
     Memory: 121.6M (peak: 123.5M)
        CPU: 3min 8.473s
     CGroup: /system.slice/lb-nginx.service
             └─runtime
               └─runtime
                 └─runtime
                   └─runtime
                     └─runtime
                       └─runtime
                         └─runtime
                           └─runtime
                             └─runtime
                               └─runtime
                                 └─runtime
                                   └─runtime
                                     └─runtime
                                       └─runtime
                                         └─runtime
                                           └─runtime
                                             └─runtime
                                               └─runtime
                                                 └─runtime
                                                   └─runtime
                                                     └─runtime
                                                       └─runtime
                                                         └─runtime
                                                           └─runtime
                                                             └─runtime
                                                               └─runtime
                                                                 └─runtime
                                                                   └─runtime
                                                                     └─runtime
                                                                       └─runtime
                                                                         └─runtime
                                                                           └─runtime
                                                                             └─runtime
                                                                               └─runtime
                                                                                 └─runtime
                                                                                   └─runtime
                                                                                     └─runtime
                                                                                       └─runtime
                                                                                         └─runtime
                                                                                           └─runtime
                                                                                             └─runtime
                                                                                               └─runtime
--- output omitted ---
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a quadlet that will constantly restart on failure. For e.g. nginx with wrong configuration.
2. Wait for around 250 nested runtime directories inside cgroup `/sys/fs/cgroup/your.service`
3. Update quadlet file
4. Do a `systemctl daemon-reload`
5. Do 'systemctl start/restart your.service`


### Describe the results you received

If you'll try to update the quadlet file (in my case I am changing image version tag to newer), reload systemd daemon you will end up with `Main process exited, code=exited, status=219/CGROUP` error

```bash
# vi /etc/containers/systemd/lb-nginx.container
# systemctl daemon-reload
# systemctl restart lb-nginx
Job for lb-nginx.service failed because the control process exited with error code.
See "systemctl status lb-nginx.service" and "journalctl -xeu lb-nginx.service" for details.
# systemctl status lb-nginx
× lb-nginx.service - lb-nginx
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/containers/systemd/lb-nginx.container; generated)
     Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Thu 2026-02-19 12:49:05 CET; 9s ago
   Duration: 11min 20.789s
    Process: 99599 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name lb-nginx --replace --rm --log-driver journald --log-opt tag=podman-lb-nginx --log-opt max-size=50mb --log-opt path=None --cgroups=split --hostname lb-ng>
    Process: 99600 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i lb-nginx (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
   Main PID: 99599 (code=exited, status=219/CGROUP)
        CPU: 38ms

Feb 19 12:49:04 myhost.local systemd[1]: Starting lb-nginx...
Feb 19 12:49:04 myhost.local systemd[1]: lb-nginx.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=219/CGROUP
Feb 19 12:49:05 myhost.local systemd[1]: lb-nginx.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Feb 19 12:49:05 myhost.local systemd[1]: Failed to start lb-nginx.
```

However if you'll do it quick enough before the runtime directories buildup too much you can update the quadlet file, reload systemd and everything will work.

I don't know if this is expected behavior for podman to build up the runtime directories or is it a cgroup/systemd bug.

The only fix for this is to:
1. Remove quadlet with `podman quadlet rm lb-nginx.container -f`
2. Reboot the server

I didn't find any way to remove this crooked cgroup. I've tried to do `systemctl reset-failed` and `systemctl daemon-reexec`

### Describe the results you expected

I want to be able to update quadlet file, reload systemd and restart my failing service.

### podman info output

```yaml

  networkBackend: netavark
  networkBackendInfo:

    dns:
      package: aardvark-dns-1.16.0-1.el9.x86_64

    package: netavark-1.16.0-1.el9.x86_64

    version: netavark 1.16.0
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun

    version: |-
      crun version 1.23.1
      commit: d20b23dba05e822b93b82f2f34fd5dada433e0c2
      rundir: /run/crun
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  pasta:
    executable: /usr/bin/pasta
    package: passt-0^20250512.g8ec1341-4.el9_7.x86_64

    rootless: false

  swapFree: 1835790336
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

It is a VM on VMware

### Additional information

N/A

comments.body:
> PodmanArgs=--restart on-failure

Do not use that option in quadlets , always use the proper systemd restart method, i.e.
```
[Service]
Restart=on-failure
```

So please make sure if that still behaves like that with the systemd way

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

@Luap99 I found out what the problem was thanks to you :)

Like you pointed out it was an issue related to using `PodmanArgs=--restart on-failure` but this was deployed by an ansible module `containers.podman.podman_container` so I didn't even notice that something is wrong, as my trust in module was too high.

After digging why the module gives bad quadlet files I found out that our ansible controller is on version 2.16, which contains `containers.podman` collection on version 1.16.2.
After updating the controller to ansible 2.20 with collection on 1.18.1 everything works as expected :) case closed

Thank you!
